name: Western Reserve Academy
about: Western Reserve Academy is an independent, coeducational boarding and day college-preparatory school in Hudson, Ohio. Founded in 1826, it is one of the oldest schools in Ohio and serves students in grades 9-12 on a 190-acre campus. The school enrolls roughly 427 students, about 64% of whom board, drawn from many states and around 15 countries.
college_matriculation: 100% of graduates attend four-year colleges; matriculations include Ivy League and other selective universities.
accreditation: Independent Schools Association of the Central States (ISACS)
notable_alumni: Macy Gray, Lincoln Ellsworth, John Hessin Clarke
financial_aid_notes: About 48% of students receive aid; the school distributes roughly $9 million in financial aid and merit awards annually, with an average grant near $36,875.

name: Oklahoma School of Science and Mathematics
about: The Oklahoma School of Science and Mathematics (OSSM) is a public, residential two-year high school in Oklahoma City serving academically advanced juniors and seniors from across Oklahoma. Established by the legislature in 1983 and opening its first class in 1990, the school is tuition-free for Oklahoma residents, with room, board, books and instruction funded by the state. All students board on its 32-acre campus and 100% are college-bound.
college_matriculation: 100% college-bound; graduates attend institutions such as MIT, Stanford, Harvard, and the University of Oklahoma; the school has produced over 500 National Merit finalists.
accreditation: Cognia; member of the National Consortium of Secondary STEM Schools (NCSSS)
financial_aid_notes: Tuition, room, board, books and class materials are fully funded by the State of Oklahoma for resident students; effectively free of charge.

name: The Kiski School
about: The Kiski School is an independent, college-preparatory boarding and day school in rural Saltsburg, Pennsylvania, about 30 miles east of Pittsburgh. Founded in 1888, it was a boys-only school for 136 years before becoming coeducational in fall 2024, and it offers grades 9-12 plus a postgraduate year. It enrolls roughly 189 students on a 350-acre campus, drawing boarders from some 31 countries.
college_matriculation: Top destinations include the U.S. Naval Academy, Penn State, Carnegie Mellon, and UNC-Chapel Hill.
notable_alumni: Bob Mathias, John Murtha, Jack Hanna
financial_aid_notes: Roughly 72% of students receive financial aid; need-based aid and merit scholarships are available, supported by a ~$12.87 million endowment.

name: Linden Hall
about: Linden Hall is an independent boarding and day school for girls in Lititz, Pennsylvania, founded in 1746 by the Moravian Church and recognized as the oldest girls' boarding school in continuous operation in the United States. It serves grades 6-12 on a 49-acre campus, enrolling roughly 123 students with about 70% boarding and a third drawn from abroad. The school offers distinctive programs including equestrian studies and aviation training.
college_matriculation: 100% college acceptance; graduates have attended schools including Yale, Princeton, Stanford, and Harvard.
financial_aid_notes: About 35% of students receive financial aid; need-based aid and merit scholarships are available.

name: Mercersburg Academy
affiliation: Independent (historic ties to the Reformed Church / United Church of Christ)
about: Mercersburg Academy is an independent, coeducational boarding and day college-preparatory school in rural Mercersburg, Pennsylvania, about 90 miles northwest of Washington, D.C. Founded in 1893, it serves grades 9-12 plus postgraduates on a 300-acre campus, enrolling roughly 452 students of whom about 81% board. The school maintains one of the higher per-student endowments among U.S. independent schools.
college_matriculation: Graduates attend a wide range of selective colleges and universities, including Ivy League institutions.
notable_alumni: James Stewart, Benicio del Toro, Burton Richter
financial_aid_notes: The school awards roughly $11-12 million in financial aid annually, with about half of families receiving merit- or need-based aid. Need-based aid is applied for through Clarity.

name: Milton Hershey School
about: Milton Hershey School is a private, tuition-free boarding school in Hershey, Pennsylvania, founded in 1909 by chocolate industrialist Milton Hershey and his wife Catherine. It serves children from lower-income families from pre-kindergarten through grade 12, providing housing, meals, education and care at no cost. Funded by a multibillion-dollar trust, it is among the wealthiest pre-college schools in the United States and enrolls roughly 2,100 students.
college_matriculation: Graduates pursue four-year colleges, two-year colleges, vocational programs, and military service with continuing scholarship support.
notable_alumni: Deesha Dyer, Trymaine Lee, Garry Gilliam
financial_aid_notes: Education is completely free; the school provides housing, meals, clothing, medical and dental care, and continuing-education scholarship support, funded by the Hershey Trust (endowment in the multibillions).
